The assignment

Questions & Sample Answers

Question 1

[1] "Why are there no boundaries between classrooms?"
 [1] A1.Because children do not get nervous.                                                                                                                                                                                   
 [2] Because the school wants children to communicate freely and move around easily.                                                                                                                                           
 [3]                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
 [4]                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
 [5] Because he thinks that children should be free and move around. He believes noise is important for children.                                                                                                              
 [6] There is no reason they get nervous because there’s no boundary.                                                                                                                                                          
 [7]                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
 [8] Because he wanted to prevent children from feeling nervous. Also, the circular design naturally guides wandering children back, while an open plan environment with ambient noise helps them concentrate and sleep better.
 [9] To eliminate anything that acoustic sounds.                                                                                                                                                                               
[10] To make no reason that children get nervous.                                                                                                                                                                              
8 Levels:  ... To make no reason that children get nervous.

Question 2

[1] "What is the average distance a child will walk or run in a morning in this school?"
 [1] The children in this school will walk or run 4000 metres on average in the morning.
 [2]                                                                                    
 [3] It                                                                                 
 [4] It’s 4kilometers                                                                   
 [5] The children in this kindergarten make 4000m on average.                           
 [6] A2.They walk or run about 4,000 meters.                                            
 [7] They walk or run about 4,000 meters every morning on average.                      
 [8] A child will walk or run about 4 kilometres in one morning.                        
 [9] 4 kilometres.                                                                      
[10] 4000 meters.                                                                       
10 Levels:  4 kilometres. ... They walk or run about 4,000 meters every morning on average.

Question 3

[1] "What kind of environments do young children like for sleeping?"
 [1] Noisy places will enable children to sleep better because it is preferable for them to sleep around the noise from the perspective of concentrating.
 [2]                                                                                                                                                     
 [3] They like noisy environment rather than a quiet space.                                                                                              
 [4] They like places with some noise around them.                                                                                                       
 [5] Young children like slightly noisy and active environments for sleeping rather than completely quiet places.                                        
 [6] The place which has noises.                                                                                                                         
 [7] A3.They sleep better in noisy places.                                                                                                               
 [8] In noise                                                                                                                                            
 [9] They like noisy places.                                                                                                                             
[10] Children sleep better in noise.                                                                                                                     
10 Levels:  ... Young children like slightly noisy and active environments for sleeping rather than completely quiet places.

Question 4:

[1] "What does the speaker think about injuries?"
 [1] They need to get some injury, for they can learn how to live in this world.                                                                                                                          
 [2] The speaker thinks small injuries are important for children. He believes children can learn how to live and become stronger through these experiences.                                              
 [3] He thinks injuries are the thing they don't have to care about too much. When someone gets injured, other children try to help them. He thinks this is the most important thing to be in this school.
 [4] Small injuries help children learn and grow.                                                                                                                                                         
 [5] The speaker thinks small injuries help children learn and grow stronger.                                                                                                                             
 [6]                                                                                                                                                                                                      
 [7] Injuries are necessary for children’s learning the way to live.                                                                                                                                      
 [8] The speaker thinks small injuries are part of childhood and help children learn and grow stronger. He believes children should not be overprotected.                                                 
 [9] The speaker thinks children should have some small dangerous experiences.                                                                                                                            
[10]                                                                                                                                                                                                      
9 Levels:  ...

Question 5:

[1] "What would be the advantages and disadvantages of sending your future child to this school?"
 [1] I think freedom can be an advantage, but sometimes could be a disadvantage.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
 [2]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 [3] It would be easy to get injured, but they can learn how to live in this world through some danger and help each other.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
 [4]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 [5] Advantages: These children have the highest athletic abilities Disadvantages: They need to tumble sometimes they need to get some injury..                                                                                                                                                                                                               
 [6]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 [7]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 [8] Advantage: Children can experience both the good and bad without stress. Disadvantage: In winter, there may be a little cold, so children may catch cold.                                                                                                                                                                                                
 [9] The advantages→The ability to build physical strength from a young age, to develop a spirit of mutual support, and to live without becoming nervous. The disadvantages→The presence of minor dangers nearby, such as the risk of injury; the stress caused by a noisy environment for children who prefer a quiet environment; and the excessive freedom.
[10] 【Adventage】 Children become active and independent. They can learn freely. 【Disadvantage】 Parents worry about safety and injuries.                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
7 Levels:  ...


Summaries

five sample summaries

[1] In this presentation, the architect Mr. Tezuka introduces a unique kindergarten he designed. The building is a two-story, circular structure with an open roof and no boundaries between inside and outside. It also has no walls dividing the classrooms. This design fosters children's growth in various ways. They can run around freely at any time, and even if they leave, the circular design naturally brings them back. Additionally, rather than enclosed, quiet spaces, they sleep and thrive better in noisy environments, just like humans originally did in the jungle. He emphasizes that in modern times, instead of controlling or overprotecting kids, it is vital to create opportunities for them to build social skills, like helping each other when they get injured.                                                                                                        
[2] He talked about a unique kindergarten. The kindergarten was constructed in 2007. It was a circle. Children who like circles can run on their roofs forever. The designs made roofs as possible , because the staff wanted to see the children on the top of roofs. The kindergarten has many water places to play, but there are no boundaries between classrooms, because if boundaries are there, the children are nervous. It is important for the children to go anytime. The speaker thinks that noise is important, so children can sleep better because of it. He attempts to change children's future.The speaker thinks that children are supported by outside.The children communicate with each other in the kindergarten activities.                                                                                                                                                     
[3] The story is about an open and airy kindergarten in Japan. Where all the children enjoy playing such as running, climbing, a tree, and playing with water. This environmental advantage is that children do not get nervous because they have to be in a classroom. Children may get injured there. The speaker thinks this is an important opportunity for children to help each other.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
[4] The video is about a unique kindergarten in Japan. The school has an open design without walls between classrooms. Children can run freely and play naturally. The architect believes children learn better when they move, explore, and interact with others. The school also uses natural spaces to help children feel comfortable and happy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
[5] In this TED talk, architect Takaharu Tezuka explains the design of a unique kindergarten in Japan. The school was created to support children's natural behavior and encourage freedom. The building is circular, allowing children to run around continuously, and there are almost no walls between classrooms. Tezuka believes that young children learn best when they can move, explore, and interact naturally. He says that children at the school run or walk about 4 kilometers every morning, which helps them stay active and healthy. The speaker also explains that children prefer noisy places for sleeping and relaxing. In addition, he believes that small injuries are an important part of growing up because children learn from experiences. Overall, the talk shows how architecture and school design can strongly influence children's development, happiness, and learning.
5 Levels: He talked about a unique kindergarten. The kindergarten was constructed in 2007. It was a circle. Children who like circles can run on their roofs forever. The designs made roofs as possible , because the staff wanted to see the children on the top of roofs. The kindergarten has many water places to play, but there are no boundaries between classrooms, because if boundaries are there, the children are nervous. It is important for the children to go anytime. The speaker thinks that noise is important, so children can sleep better because of it. He attempts to change children's future.The speaker thinks that children are supported by outside.The children communicate with each other in the kindergarten activities. ...


Plagiarism Check

What was the longest string of words shared among the students’ summaries and transcript?

Comment: One student has copied herself. Also it’s a memorable chunk of English. (By the way, he should have said “injuries”, or even better and more natural “hurt sometimes”.)

word count for summary


Opinions

five sample opinions

[1] I thought this TED Talk was very interesting because it showed a different way of thinking about education. Many schools focus too much on rules and quiet classrooms, but Fuji Kindergarten allows children to move and explore naturally. I think this is important because young children learn through experience and play. The open environment may help children become more confident and independent. I also liked the idea that small risks can help children grow stronger mentally and physically. However, this kind of school may be difficult to build in every country because of cost and safety concerns. Even so, I believe schools around the world can learn from Tezuka’s ideas and try to create happier and more creative learning spaces for children.
[2]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
[3] Watching this video reminded me of growing up in a daycare center where the playground was very small; I spent most of my time making mud dumplings and practicing back hip circles on the horizontal bar. I think the architectural design of this kindergarten is truly wonderful. I always wanted to climb trees when I was little, and here, children can do that every day. A completely free space that still maintains a certain level of safety is perfect for children. Being able to move their body to their heart's content whenever they feel like it has such a positive impact on kids.                                                                                                                                                                        
[4] I agree with this video. I think a small dosage of dander is very good for children. My nursery school was in Tokyo, and it had a very small playground. Therefore, playing outside was not very fun for me. In this video, the children looked very happy. They were enjoying playing outside. I believe that making mistakes and experiencing small dangers are necessary for kids.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
[5]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
4 Levels:  ...
